_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Need for Prenatal Diagnosis of Genetic Disorders
min_doctor_qualification: (DM/Equivalent in Genetics) 1 Year training in maternal fetal medicine/PDCC/Equivalent certificate course
clinical_indications: Prenatal diagnosis of genetic disorders especially in prenatal screen positive women

clinical_thresholds.note: indicating need for fetal genetic analysis

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission

claim.label: Nuchal translucency (NT) and Early TIFFA (Targeted imaging for fetal anomalies) scan reports

ppd_preauth.q: Was the prenatal screen of the women positive indicating need for fetal genetic analysis?

cpd_claim.q: Did the NT/Early TIFFA scan reports submitted show any abnormality and indicative of performing the procedure?

common_queries: Prenatal screen of the women was not positive or did not indicate a need for fetal genetic analysis
NT/Early TIFFA scan reports did not show any abnormality or were not indicative of performing the procedure
Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization
Nuchal translucency (NT) and Early TIFFA scan reports are missing or incomplete at claim submission
Detailed procedure/operative notes are missing or incomplete at claim submission

additional_information.special_empanelment: Care at Tertiary hospital, procedure done under ultrasound guidance. Facility should be registered as per PCPNDT law for intervention procedures.
clinical_key_pointers: Chorionic Villus Sampling (CVS) is performed for prenatal diagnosis of genetic disorders especially in prenatal screen positive women.
The procedure is carried out transcervically (TC) and transabdominally.
This procedure is of low risks, technically easier and cytogenetic results are obtained within 24-48 hours
Time: Transcervical 10-13 weeks, Transabdominal 10 weeks to term
Karyotype result: Direct preparation: 24-48 hours. Culture: 10-14 days
Accuracy: Accurate; may need amniocentesis for confirmation
Termination of pregnancy when indicated: 1st trimester—safe
Maternal effects following termination of pregnancy: Very little
False-positive results (2–3%) are there due to placental mosaics and maternal cell contamination. In such a situation, amniocentesis should be performed to confirm the diagnosis.
Limb reduction deformity (LRD) is low when CVS is performed after 9 completed weeks of gestation.
contraindications: Uterine malformations or in presence of infections, such as the genital herpes or cervicitis
